seoTitle: How to Use Grammarly in Google Docs: 2026 Setup Guide
metaDescription: See how to install Grammarly, review real-time suggestions, and adjust writing goals inside Google Docs so your writing stays clear and error-free.
intro: Grammarly adds real-time grammar, spelling, and clarity checks right inside your Google Docs editing window. This guide covers installing the extension, understanding the floating widget, and tuning your writing profile so the suggestions actually match your tone and audience. It's a quick setup that pays off on every document you write afterward.

steps.heading: Install the Grammarly extension
steps.body: Head to your browser's extension store, search for Grammarly, and install the official assistant. Once added, it runs in the background across your Google Workspace tabs.

steps.heading: Find the floating widget in your document
steps.body: Open a Google Doc and look for the Grammarly icon that appears inside the document canvas. This floating widget is your entry point to suggestions and settings.

steps.heading: Review real-time suggestions
steps.body: As you type, color-coded feedback highlights spelling, grammar, and sentence-structure issues. Click a suggestion to accept it instantly, or open the sidebar for more detailed rewrites.

steps.heading: Set your writing goals
steps.body: Adjust your target audience, formality level, and tone in your writing profile so Grammarly's suggestions match the kind of document you're producing, whether that's casual or formal.

steps.heading: Keep the workflow lightweight
steps.body: Use the persistent assistant for ongoing proofreading, but toggle it off when you want a clutter-free view, then turn it back on before a final pass.

faq.question: Does Grammarly work with the free version of Google Docs?
faq.answer: Yes, the Grammarly browser extension integrates with the free, standard Google Docs interface once it's installed and enabled.

faq.question: Can I turn off Grammarly suggestions temporarily?
faq.answer: Yes, you can pause or disable checking through the floating widget's toggle without uninstalling the extension entirely.

faq.question: Will Grammarly change my writing tone automatically?
faq.answer: No, it only suggests changes based on the tone and formality goals you set. You choose whether to accept each suggestion.

faq.question: Does the free version of Grammarly cover everything I need?
faq.answer: The free tier covers core spelling, grammar, and punctuation fixes, while advanced clarity and engagement scoring may require a paid plan.
